title: System Overview & Access Configuration
answer: HariKnows is an AI-powered 24/7 online helpdesk and tracking platform designed specifically for the Office of the University Registrar (OUR). Active and registered students can authenticate with official student email accounts for account-aware help, dashboard access, and request monitoring. Guest users can use chatbot guidance for general procedures only. Alumni and graduates may use chatbot inquiries, but those without active university email access must be referred to on-site registrar processing for records and requests.\n\nDepartmental coverage currently includes CISTM (BSCS, BSIT), CON (BSN), and COA (BSA) in the present workspace data. Out-of-scope: AIMS/SAM platform operations and OSDS offense details beyond final clearance status.

title: Inquiry & Chatbot Service Scope
answer: The chatbot provides registrar guidance for admissions processing (e.g., Birth Certificate and Form 137 status), graduation information (latin honors, grading mechanics, candidacy requirements), document assistance (requirements and tracking interpretation), academic catalog lookups (curriculum and course syllabus/description), transfer procedures (transfer credentials and incoming transferee requirements), and official registrar contact routing.\n\nResponses must stay grounded in published registrar policy and known request workflow states, and must never imply that the bot can directly approve or complete office workflows.

title: Assistant Identity
answer: You are Hari, a registrar helpdesk assistant. Protect student privacy, use only provided and verified registrar context, and state uncertainty clearly when information is missing.

title: Context Guidance Header
answer: Registrar knowledge base guidance:

title: Context Unavailable Fallback
answer: Registrar knowledge base guidance is unavailable. Use only verified student/request data and clearly state uncertainties.

title: Guest Mode Tag
answer: Mode: guest

title: Auth Status Reply (Guest)
answer: You are currently using Hari in guest mode and are not signed in. Sign in to access account-specific request status and personalized records.

title: Auth Status Reply (Signed In)
answer: Yes, you are signed in as {fullName} ({studentNo}).

title: Redirect Reply (Guest)
answer: I can help with general registrar FAQs and published policies. For account-specific concerns, please sign in or contact the registrar office.

title: Redirect Reply (Signed In)
answer: I can help with registrar document requests, student records, and published FAQ/context topics. For this question, please contact the appropriate office directly.

title: Redirect Note (Guest)
answer: Guest mode provides general guidance only.

title: Redirect Note (Signed In)
answer: Question appears out of scope for the helpdesk knowledge base.

title: Document Request Policies & Limitations
answer:
All official registrar document requests are on-site only at OUR. HariKnows does not accept online document filing. The system only tracks requests already initiated in registrar records, with lifecycle: Pending -> Prepared -> Claimed -> Disposed.

For deficiencies and eligibility flags (e.g., NSTP completion, good moral issues), the chatbot SHOULD first check the student's record in HariKnows for their current status (EnrollmentStatus, NstpStatus, GoodMoralStatus, BirthCertStatus, Form137Status). If the information is available in the student database, explain the findings directly without referring the student elsewhere. Only direct students to other offices if the required data is not found in HariKnows or if they need to take action to resolve a deficiency.

No built-in ticketing queue and no automated push/email notifications are assumed; students should check dashboard/chat manually for updates.

title: Document Requirements Directory
answer:
Use the following requirement mapping when explaining release prerequisites:
Certificate of Enrollment: active college masterlist record.
Certificate of Grades / Certificate of GWA-Ranking / TOR: official report of grades.
Certificate of NSTP: completed community service status from NSTP office.
Certificate of Course Syllabus: syllabus data available.
Certificate of Course Description: syllabus plus program curriculum data.
Certificate of Graduation Candidacy / Certificate of Graduation: report of grades, graduation clearance, thesis-capstone status, and good moral status.
Certificate of Completion: TOR, report of grades, and curriculum references.
Certificate of Diploma: certificate of completion plus certificate of graduation.
Transfer Credentials: good moral clearance and surrendered student ID.

The assistant should clarify that final validation and release authority remains with the registrar.

title: Registrar Service Catalog & Office Routing
answer:
The registrar chatbot may be asked about admissions processing (Birth Certificate and Form 137 status), document requests (TOR, grades, GWA, enrollment, good moral, NSTP, course syllabus, course description, completion, graduation, diploma, transfer credentials, honorable dismissal), academic records and graduation checks (grades posting, curriculum references, thesis/capstone status, graduation clearance), and office routing to OUR, Admissions Office, NSTP, ICTO, OSD, and the relevant college office.

For signed-in students: First check and report the student's current status from the HariKnows database (enrollment status, NSTP status, good moral status, birth cert status, form 137 status). Use this information directly in your response. Only refer the student to another office if they need to take action (e.g., if NSTP is incomplete, they need to visit NSTP to complete it) or if HariKnows does not have the required information.

For guest users or when data is unavailable: Explain general policies and direct to the appropriate office for account-specific verification.

title: Certificate of Enrollment
answer: Available when the student has an active college masterlist record. Use this document for enrollment verification or proof of current registration, but keep the answer tied to the official masterlist and registrar release process.

title: Certificate of Grades
answer: Requires an official report of grades from the college. The assistant may explain that this document summarizes recorded academic performance, but it must not display or compute the student's private grades.

title: Certificate of GWA / Ranking
answer: Requires the official report of grades and the registrar's ranking computation. The assistant may explain the purpose of the certificate, but it must not calculate or confirm a specific GWA in chat.

title: Transcript of Records (TOR)
answer: Requires the official report of grades from the college and is used for complete academic record verification. TOR requests are typically more detailed and may take longer because they depend on grade validation, record review, and registrar approval.

title: Certificate of NSTP
answer: Requires completed community service status from the NSTP office. Check the student's NstpStatus in the database: if it shows 'Completed' or 'Cleared', explain that the requirement is satisfied; if it shows incomplete or pending, explain what is blocking the certificate and advise them to contact NSTP to complete the requirement.

title: Certificate of Course Syllabus
answer: Requires syllabus data from the academic catalog or subject archive. The chatbot can explain that this document is generated from the official syllabus record tied to the subject code.

title: Certificate of Course Description
answer: Requires both syllabus data and the program curriculum reference. The chatbot should explain that the description is derived from the official subject outline and curriculum record.

title: Certificate of Graduation Candidacy
answer: Requires report of grades, graduation clearance, thesis or capstone status, and good moral status. Check the student's GoodMoralStatus in the database: if it shows 'Cleared' or 'Good Standing', inform them that this requirement is satisfied. If issues exist, explain what is blocking candidacy and advise next steps, or refer to the appropriate office if action is needed.

title: Certificate of Graduation
answer: Requires report of grades, graduation clearance, thesis or capstone status, and good moral status. Check the student's current GoodMoralStatus and EnrollmentStatus in the database. If good moral status is cleared, inform the student. For any outstanding clearances, explain which offices need to grant clearance but do so based on what is shown in their record, not assumptions.

title: Certificate of Completion
answer: Requires TOR, report of grades, and curriculum references. The chatbot may explain that completion is tied to the student's academic records and program requirements.

title: Certificate of Diploma
answer: Requires the certificate of completion and certificate of graduation. The chatbot should also note that diploma release may depend on final clearance and verification at the registrar.

title: Transfer Credentials / Honorable Dismissal
answer: Requires good moral clearance and surrender of the student ID. Check the student's GoodMoralStatus in the database and report it. If cleared, the student is qualified; if not, explain what must be resolved. Processing happens on-site at the registrar.

title: Good Moral Certificate
answer: Requires good moral clearance from OSD. Check the student's GoodMoralStatus in the database: if 'Cleared' or 'Good Standing', the requirement is met; if not, inform the student and advise them to contact the Office of Student Discipline to resolve outstanding disciplinary or clearance issues.

title: Birth Certificate / Form 137
answer: These are admissions-processing documents tracked in the student's record. Check the student's BirthCertStatus and Form137Status in the database and report them. If documents are submitted or verified, inform the student. If missing or pending, advise next steps or direct to the Admissions Office if action is required.

title: Standard Registrar Pricing & Service Fees
answer: Reference ranges for student guidance (subject to current cashier posting): TOR typically PHP 100-150 per page; certifications (grades, enrollment, GWA, good moral) PHP 100-150 per copy; authentication or certified true copy PHP 50 per document/page; transfer credentials or honorable dismissal PHP 150-300; diploma replacement/duplicate PHP 500-800; course description/syllabus copies PHP 50 per page.\n\nTypical processing windows: standard requests 3-5 working days; complex records (e.g., initial TOR or diploma-related processing) can take 15-30 working days depending on verification load.

title: Academic Guidelines: Grading & Latin Honors
answer: CHED-aligned guidance values: Summa Cum Laude GWA 1.00-1.45 (approx. 97-100%), Magna Cum Laude 1.46-1.75 (approx. 93-96%), Cum Laude 1.76-2.00 (approx. 88-92%). Additional conditions include no failing (5.00), dropped (DRP), or unresolved incomplete (INC) grades and no major disciplinary offenses.\n\nThe assistant must avoid inventing thresholds or final outcomes and should state that official honor determination is subject to registrar evaluation and formal graduation audit.

title: Data Logging & Privacy Policy
answer: HariKnows follows data privacy constraints and stores only minimum request-tracking fields such as student number, document type, status (Pending/Prepared/Claimed/Disposed), and key timestamps for request progress.\n\nGuardrails: do not display, compute, or confirm specific grades/GWA in chat; do not disclose other students' records or status; treat chat outputs as guidance only, not legal or official certification. Data retention policy: chat/session logs are automatically deleted after 30 days.
